question: What is late timing?
content:
Timing is correct only if, when the slide foot is planted, the ball is still on its way down (near-parallel)—not already at the ankle (early) and not still behind the body (late).
The correction to the “ball + slide foot together” myth
If someone says: “Perfect timing is when the ball and sliding foot arrive together.”
Your answer is:

No. Ball + slide foot together = early timing.
Perfect timing = the slide foot plants first, then the ball arrives.
Feet finish first; the ball finishes last.
